data.title: BTCFi TVL soars to $7B, with trust concerns despite 22x surge from Jan 2024, driven by protocols & institutional investment.
data.description: Bitcoin-based decentralized finance, BTCFi, has seen a significant increase of more than 2200% in its Total Value Locked (TVL) since January 2024. This drastic surge is attributed to the introduction of new protocols and a notable increase in institutional investments. However, despite this growth, BTCFi continues to encounter challenges in adoption primarily due to concerns surrounding trust within the platform.

data.title: Battery Ventures promotes Justin Rosner to partner, announces a dozen internal promotions. Also promotes four new principals.
data.description: Battery Ventures, an international technology investment firm, recently revealed a significant announcement regarding internal promotions. The firm, with offices in Boston, New York, San Francisco, and Tel Aviv in Israel, disclosed nearly twelve advancements within its team. Notably, Justin Rosner, known for expertise in industrial technology and life-science tools, was promoted to the position of partner. Additionally, Battery appointed four new principals within its venture-capital and private-equity sectors, showcasing its commitment to nurturing talent from within. This move underscores the firm's dedication to recognizing and advancing its professionals, contributing to its strong leadership foundation.

data.title: PNC Financial Services reported Q2 earnings of $3.85 per share, beating estimates of $3.53 per share. Quarterly sales were $5.661 billion, exceeding analyst expectations of $5.606 billion, prompting analysts to raise forecasts.CEO expects FY 2025 revenue to increase by 6%.
data.description: PNC Financial Services Group, Inc. (NYSE: PNC) announced impressive second-quarter earnings surpassing expectations. Quarterly earnings stood at $3.85 per share, outperforming the estimated $3.53 per share. Moreover, the company's quarterly sales reached $5.661 billion, beating the anticipated $5.606 billion. PNC foresees a 6% annual revenue growth for FY 2025. Bill Demchak, Chairman and CEO of PNC, highlighted the success of the national growth strategy with an increase in new customer acquisition. For more information, the complete news is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: HealthQuest Capital adds Terry Shaw, former AdventHealth CEO, to Industry Advisory Board in 2025.
data.description: On July 17, 2025, in San Francisco, HealthQuest Capital, a private asset firm supporting healthcare companies, announced Terry Shaw, the former President and CEO of AdventHealth, as the newest member of its Industry Advisory Board. This addition brings valuable expertise to the board, enhancing its ability to guide and support transformative healthcare ventures.

data.title: HTX's 2025 Mid-Year Report: 50M users, $6.4B assets, hot crypto's 850% surge. Crypto market shifts ongoing.
data.description: In Panama City on July 17, 2025, HTX, a prominent international cryptocurrency exchange, is facing a volatile crypto market as the year reaches its midpoint. The market is fluctuating due to conflicting forces of regulation and technological developments. Bitcoin has shown significant growth, surpassing key levels...

data.title: ETH surged 7% to $3,400, up 24% in a week, targeting $4,000, driven by ETF flows and institutional interest.
data.description: Ethereum (ETH) has surged by 24% in the last week due to increased ETF investments, clearer regulations, and revived institutional interest. The cryptocurrency is approaching a crucial resistance level, with traders anticipating a potential breakout to the $4,000 mark. Analyst Benjamin Cowen believes that ETH may have hit its cycle low against Bitcoin (BTC) and is now following its long-term logarithmic regression trendline. This could signify the beginning of a sustained upward trend for ETH. However, Cowen advises caution against extensive exposure to alternative coins, drawing comparisons to previous market cycles like those seen in 2017 and 2019. For more details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: TWG to consolidate shares at 1-for-90 ratio; Class A and B ordinary shares to merge under US0.0001 par value each.
data.description: In Hong Kong on July 17, 2025, Top Wealth Group Holding Limited (NASDAQ: TWG) disclosed the approval of a planned 1-for-90 share consolidation of their Class A and Class B ordinary shares. The consolidation pertains to the Ordinary Shares, valued at US0.0001 par each.

data.title: Super League and Advertising Week are teaming up for a gaming summit at AWNewYork, focusing on playable media, brand innovation, and consumer engagement.
data.description: A forum lasting half a day will focus on upcoming trends in playable media, brand innovation, and next-generation consumer engagement. This event will feature discussions on the latest developments in these areas.

data.title: TWG signs MOU with Xiuzheng Group for pet pharmaceutical products. Business deal announced in Hong Kong on July 17, 2025.
data.description: On July 17, 2025, Top Wealth Group Holding Limited (NASDAQ: TWG) disclosed a significant development, reporting its formal agreement with Jilin Xiuzheng Agriculture and Animal Husbandry Technology Co., Ltd. The memorandum of understanding (MOU) outlines their collaborative efforts in the injection of animal-related pharmaceutical products, food, and supplements. Jilin Xiuzheng, a part of the esteemed Xiuzheng Group, represents a noteworthy player in the Chinese pharmaceutical industry. This partnership signals Top Wealth's strategic move into the animal market sector encompassing pets and livestock, demonstrating their long-term vision and commitment to diversification.

data.title: Decentralized exchange surpasses centralized ones as traders embrace decentralization, but CEXs dominate with $3.9T volume vs DEXs' $877B.
data.description: Even though the DEX-to-CEX ratio is on the rise, centralized exchanges continue to dominate the cryptocurrency spot market. They generated a trading volume of $3.9 trillion, while decentralized exchanges (DEXs) recorded $877 billion in trading volume.

data.title: ITS Logistics Port Rail Ramp Index for July 2025 reports stable port operations but uncertainty due to rising project freight imports. US container volume for June increased by 1.8%, Port of Los Angeles had its busiest June with 8% higher volumes than in 2024. Contention, trucking, equipment availability, and distribution centers are at normal level absorption of inbound container flow.
data.description: ITS Logistics released the July forecast for the ITS Logistics US Port/Rail Ramp Freight Index, showing most US ports operating normally but smaller ports facing strain from rising project freight imports. Container volumes hit record highs at the Port of Los Angeles in June, with overall US container volume for June at 2,217,675 TEUs, up 1.8% month-over-month. The Port of Los Angeles saw an 8% increase from 2024 volumes, making it the busiest June on record. However, uncertainties remain due to frontloading trends, tariff volatility, nearshoring efforts, and domestic manufacturing, which could strain specialized equipment capacity for hazmat, oversized, and breakbulk cargo. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Goldman Sachs surpasses Q2 estimates with $14.58B in net revenue, $10.91 EPS, and first-half EPS of $25.07. Analysts increase forecasts.
data.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. (NYSE: GS) exceeded analyst expectations in its second-quarter report, with a 15% increase in net revenue year over year to $14.58 billion. This exceeded the consensus estimate of $13.36 billion but represented a 3% decrease from the previous quarter. GAAP earnings per share stood at $10.91, compared to $8.62 the previous year, surpassing the consensus of $9.48. First-half earnings per share rose to $25.07 from $20.21 the previous year. David Solomon, the Chairman and CEO of Goldman Sachs, noted that the economy and markets are showing positive responses.
